threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can tell a newbie by how they look for the 243 bus stop instead of just walking the two blocks to the train. Classic rookie mistake.
Caught the N68 last night, and the driver started singing along to classic soul. Had the whole back of the bus in stitches. Only in Brixton!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids trading Pokémon cards on the sidewalk while a couple argued passionately in Turkish next door. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know it’s Berlin when you see a crowd of people dancing on the U-Bahn platform during a strike. The city's chaos has a certain rhythm!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know the best taco trucks have lines that start well before noon, while newcomers just hit the trendy taquerias they read about online.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy arguing with the Muni driver about the route while juggling a pizza. Classic SF chaos, you can’t make this stuff up.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's plazas are for relaxing, but locals know they're just waiting areas for fútbol fans during match days.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still struggling with the ‘hola’ and ‘gràcies’ while fumbling with their mapa, lost in the winding alleys of Gràcia.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the ‘Overground’ instead of just ‘the train’ and look confused when locals refer to ‘the bus’ with a number.
Heard a guy on the 196 bus raving about how the DJ at Electric Avenue used to mix just like his dad. You could feel the pride in his voice.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy playing the accordion while juggling flaming torches at Hermannplatz. That's Neukölln in a nutshell.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a café where customers pay based on how they feel that day. The honor system really brings out the best in people.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their ability to navigate the BART delays while still clutching their burrito. Tourists just look lost.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full Santa suit debating with a pigeon on Powell Street. Pigeon won, flew off with an abandoned french fry. Classic Muni.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about the parks and plazas, but it’s really about the tiny, hidden bars where you hear real Catalan gossip over cheap vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still taking photos of the Casa Ramos and asking locals for directions to Park Güell like it’s off the beaten path.
